description: Add Open Graph and Twitter Card meta tags to the root layout and /roadmap page so shared links render with proper title, description, and preview image on social platforms.

userStories.title: Create OG image and add Open Graph + Twitter Card metadata to root layout
userStories.description: As a user sharing the app URL on social media, I want the link to render with a proper title, description, and preview image so the app looks professional when shared.
acceptanceCriteria: Create a placeholder OG image at public/og-image.png (1200x630 pixels) — a simple branded placeholder with 'OpenHarness' text, kept under 100KB
Root layout exports metadata with openGraph fields: og:title, og:description, og:url (https://next-postgres-shadcn.ruska.dev), og:site_name (OpenHarness), og:type (website)
Root layout metadata includes openGraph.images with url (absolute: https://next-postgres-shadcn.ruska.dev/og-image.png), width (1200), height (630), alt text
Root layout exports metadata with twitter object: card ('summary_large_image'), title, description
Typecheck passes (npm run type-check)
Lint passes (npm run lint)
Format check passes (npm run format:check)
Verify in browser using agent-browser skill — navigate to homepage and confirm page renders
userStories.notes: Use Next.js Metadata API — extend the existing metadata export in src/app/layout.tsx. For the placeholder image, use a simple approach: create a minimal valid PNG programmatically or commit a pre-made asset. OG image must be under 100KB. Use the twitter: { card, title, description } object shape (not flat twitter:card keys).

userStories.title: Add page-specific OG metadata to /roadmap page
userStories.description: As a user sharing the roadmap URL, I want the shared link to show roadmap-specific title and description rather than inheriting generic root metadata.
acceptanceCriteria: /roadmap page exports metadata with openGraph fields: og:title ('Roadmap | OpenHarness'), og:description (roadmap-specific), og:url (https://next-postgres-shadcn.ruska.dev/roadmap)
/roadmap page exports metadata with twitter object: card ('summary_large_image'), title, description
OG image is inherited from root layout (no need to override)
Typecheck passes (npm run type-check)
Lint passes (npm run lint)
Format check passes (npm run format:check)
Verify in browser using agent-browser skill — navigate to /roadmap and check page renders correctly
userStories.notes: Extend the existing metadata export in src/app/roadmap/page.tsx. Next.js merges page metadata with layout metadata, so only override what's different (title, description, url). The og:image will be inherited from root.

userStories.title: Add tests for OG metadata configuration
userStories.description: As a developer, I need tests verifying OG metadata is correctly configured to catch regressions.
acceptanceCriteria: Create test file for metadata verification (e.g. src/__tests__/metadata.test.ts)
Test: root layout metadata includes openGraph.title, openGraph.description, openGraph.url, openGraph.siteName
Test: root layout metadata includes twitter.card = 'summary_large_image'
Test: root layout metadata openGraph.images[0] has url containing '/og-image.png', width 1200, height 630
Test: roadmap page metadata includes openGraph.title containing 'Roadmap'
All tests pass (npm test)
Typecheck passes (npm run type-check)
Format check passes (npm run format:check)
userStories.notes: Import the metadata exports directly from layout.tsx and roadmap/page.tsx and assert on their properties. No need for HTTP requests — just test the exported objects.

userStories.title: Archive Ralph run, validate app, submit draft PR, and verify CI green
userStories.description: As the agent, I archive the Ralph run, validate the app is serving, submit all work as a draft PR with review docs, and confirm CI passes.
acceptanceCriteria: All previous stories have passes: true
Archive .ralph/prd.json and .ralph/progress.txt to .ralph/archives/2026-04-08/og-meta-tags/ (plural archives, date and feature as SEPARATE directories)
Verify archive exists: ls .ralph/archives/2026-04-08/og-meta-tags/prd.json must succeed
Verify dev server: curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' http://localhost:3000/ must return 200 — if not running, start with npm run dev and wait
Verify public URL: curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' https://next-postgres-shadcn.ruska.dev/ must return 200 — if 502/000, this is a BLOCKER
Create feature branch: git checkout -b feat/5-og-meta-tags from agent/next-postgres-shadcn (NEVER git clone)
Push all commits to the feature branch

Create draft PR to development with body containing: Closes #5 (MUST be on its own line so GitHub auto-closes the issue on merge), ## Roadmap Context (Rank: 2, Category: docs, Phase: now, Complexity: S, Signal: infrastructure), ## What (summary of all changes made across stories), ## Why (motivation — link to roadmap item and GitHub issue, explain the infrastructure signal that justified building this), ## How (implementation approach, key architecture decisions, tradeoffs), ## Manual Review Steps (numbered checklist: specific pages to visit, interactions to test, edge cases to verify — written for a human reviewer who has NOT seen the code), ## Acceptance Criteria (consolidated from all user stories)
PR body MUST include 'Closes #5' where 5 is the GitHub issue number — this auto-closes the issue when the PR merges
PR body MUST include a Roadmap Context section with: Rank: 2, Category: docs, Phase: now, Complexity: S, Signal: infrastructure — matching the pinned roadmap issue exactly
PR title follows format: feat(#5): add Open Graph meta tags and social sharing cards
Run /ci-status after push — poll until CI completes
If CI fails: read failure logs, fix the issue, push again, re-poll until GREEN
This story is NOT complete until CI pipeline is GREEN AND public URL returns 200 — do not mark passes: true otherwise
userStories.notes: ALWAYS the last story. Archive to .ralph/archives/2026-04-08/og-meta-tags/. Dev server + public URL MUST be verified. CI MUST be green. PR body MUST have Closes #5 and Roadmap Context.
